title: Folder tree: INBOX and its subfolders appear twice when accounts have case-differing inbox names
author: XtraLarge
body:
Hi, and thanks a lot for mail-archiver!

I ran into a folder-tree display glitch on a multi-account, fairly large archive and did a bit of digging that might save you time. It's purely a tree-rendering thing — the stored data is completely fine (every folder is a single distinct row).

What I see
In the combined ("all accounts") folder tree, an INBOX folder and all of its year subfolders show up twice.

Minimal repro
- Account A has a folder named "INBOX"
- Account B has a folder named "Inbox"  (same name, only differs in case)
- Account C has dovecot-style subfolders "INBOX.2024", "INBOX.2025", ...
- Open the combined folder tree → "INBOX" appears twice, each copy carrying the same year subfolders.

Root cause (in GetFolderTreeAsync)
- The tree is built across all accounts, grouped by folder name only.
- allNodes and folderNameSet use StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase, so "INBOX" (account A) and "Inbox" (account B) collapse onto the same node.
- Both names still exist as separate entries in validFolderData, so in the node-placement loop the same (merged) node object gets added to the root list twice.
- The parent scan also treats '.' as a hierarchy separator, so "INBOX.2024" etc. attach to that shared "INBOX" node — and therefore render under both duplicated copies.

Net effect: INBOX + its subfolders are shown twice. Selecting a single account shows everything correctly (the year folders appear once), which confirms it's tree construction, not data.

Possible direction
Building the tree per account (so folders from different accounts aren't merged or cross-parented) would fix both the duplication and the cross-account nesting. A lighter touch would be to match/parent case-sensitively and only within the same account. Happy to open a PR if that would help — just let me know what you'd prefer.

Environment: version 2607.2, PostgreSQL, several IMAP accounts.

Thanks again!

title: M365/Graph accounts never get RawHeaders — In-Reply-To/References threading headers permanently missing
author: fhalamzie
body:
## Summary

For mailboxes synced via the Microsoft Graph (M365) provider, `ArchivedEmails.RawHeaders` is never populated — 0% across every M365 account we have, regardless of age. IMAP-synced mailboxes are unaffected (100% populated). As a result, `In-Reply-To`/`References` (and any other raw header) are permanently unavailable for Graph-synced mail, which breaks email-threading downstream for anyone consuming the archive.

## Environment

- Image: `s1t5/mailarchiver:latest`
- Release: 2607.2 (2026-07-09, rev `11c3ebcd`) — confirmed current as of this report
- Postgres 17

## Evidence

Query against `mail_archiver."ArchivedEmails"` joined to `"MailAccounts"`, restricted to `"SentDate" > 2026-06-01` (results are the same shape across the full archive, this window is just illustrative):

```sql
SELECT ma."Provider", COUNT(*) AS emails,
       COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE ae."RawHeaders" IS NOT NULL AND ae."RawHeaders" != '') AS with_raw_headers
FROM mail_archiver."ArchivedEmails" ae
JOIN mail_archiver."MailAccounts" ma ON ma."Id" = ae."MailAccountId"
WHERE ae."SentDate" > '2026-06-01'
GROUP BY 1;
```

| Provider | accounts | emails | `RawHeaders` populated |
|---|---|---|---|
| IMAP | 15 | 1,880 | 1,880 (**100%**) |
| M365 (Graph) | 9 | 1,878 | 0 (**0%**) |

Every one of our 9 Graph-provider mailboxes has zero rows with a populated `RawHeaders`. IMAP is fully fine.

## Suggested fix

The Graph `message` resource already exposes raw internet headers via the `internetMessageHeaders` property on the same `GET /messages/{id}` call the Graph sync path presumably already makes — add `internetMessageHeaders` to the `$select` list and persist the returned `{name, value}` pairs (or fold them into a `RawHeaders`-equivalent blob, matching what the IMAP path already stores). No extra API round-trip, no MIME parsing needed.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/resources/message

Happy to help verify against our own M365 accounts once a fix is up.

title: [Bug]: OIDC login events log the reverse-proxy IP instead of the real client IP (logout logs it correctly)
author: djanzin
body:
### Bug Description

Auth: OIDC enabled, external IdP (Authentik)
Reverse proxy: Caddy, sets X-Forwarded-For and X-Real-IP to the real client IP
App reached only via the proxy (ASPNETCORE_URLS=http://+:5000)
Summary
In the activity log, OIDC login events record the reverse proxy's internal IP (e.g. ::ffff:10.0.0.2), while logout events and failed-login events record the correct real client IP. So the client IP is captured inconsistently depending on the code path.

Evidence (activity log, IPs anonymized)
timestamp   user      type       information
21:34       <user>    Login      IP: ::ffff:10.0.0.2      <- reverse proxy IP
21:33       <user>    Logout     IP: <real client IP>     <- correct
21:33       <user>    Login      IP: ::ffff:10.0.0.2      <- reverse proxy IP
21:24       <user>    Logout     IP: <real client IP>     <- correct
Failed local logins also log the correct real client IP.

Analysis
The application already uses UseForwardedHeaders and clearly trusts the proxy (KnownProxies/KnownNetworks), because logout and failed-login events resolve to the real client IP. If the proxy were not trusted, every event would show the proxy IP, which is not the case.

The login path is the outlier. This strongly suggests a middleware ordering issue: the OIDC callback / authentication middleware captures and logs the client IP before UseForwardedHeaders has rewritten HttpContext.Connection.RemoteIpAddress. Regular requests (logout) pass through the full pipeline first, so they see the corrected IP.

Expected behavior
OIDC login events should log the real client IP, consistent with logout and failed-login events.

Suggested fix
Ensure app.UseForwardedHeaders(...) runs before the authentication middleware (and before any OIDC sign-in event logging) in the pipeline, so the corrected RemoteIpAddress is available when the login event is recorded.

Note: cause inferred from behavior plus a symbol scan of MailArchiver.dll, not from source, so the exact call site may differ.

### Steps to Reproduce

Run Mail-Archiver behind a reverse proxy that sets X-Forwarded-For / X-Real-IP to the real client IP (here: Caddy), with the app only reachable through the proxy.
Enable OIDC with an external IdP and log in via the OIDC button.
Open the activity log and look at the Login entry: the IP shown is the reverse proxy's internal IP (::ffff:10.0.0.2), not the client's.
Log out, then look at the Logout entry: the IP shown is the real client IP.
Compare both entries for the same session, the IPs are inconsistent.

### Application Version

2607.2

### Installation Type

Standalone

### Management Tools

_No response_

### Host System

_No response_

### Mail Provider

_No response_

### Relevant Log Output

```shell
Evidence (activity log, IPs anonymized)
timestamp   user      type       information
21:34       <user>    Login      IP: ::ffff:10.0.0.2      <- reverse proxy IP
21:33       <user>    Logout     IP: <real client IP>     <- correct
21:33       <user>    Login      IP: ::ffff:10.0.0.2      <- reverse proxy IP
21:24       <user>    Logout     IP: <real client IP>     <- correct
```

title: [Bug]: Weird Issues (Renaming Account & Initial Sync)
author: polskikrol
body:
### Bug Description

So, see some oddity.
1) I had a sync job happening, and I just wanted to rename the account, not change it or anything. Well, renaming it appears to have stopped the sync job, and created a new one from scratch. It has been working on my large Inbox folder overnight I presume? I would think that renaming an account should not impact any jobs, as it is just a label. Also, if the underlying email account is the same, shouldnt the sync take less time, as you would presumably already have a large bulk of the emails? Or are we literally downloading everything again and comparing via MD5 sum or something?
2) Added an additional account and sync completed. Checking archive -> filtering on it, I see 2026 emails. On the dashboard the storage shows 0.0B which is not possible. Also, at this point in time on Dashboard, Storage is 7.8GB, the account I renamed is at 9.9GB and the new account I added is at 0.0B. I would have *presumed* that the dashboard number atop would be the sum of the individual email accounts listed in table below?

I am thinking some the way we determine email storage usage has to be reworked a bit. There are a few other suggestions here as well: https://github.com/s1t5/mail-archiver/discussions/418.

### Steps to Reproduce

Not applicable.

### Application Version

2607.1

### Installation Type

Docker

### Management Tools

_No response_

### Host System

_No response_

### Mail Provider

_No response_

### Relevant Log Output

```shell
N/A
```

title: [Bug]: Sync Progress Bar based on completed folders?
author: polskikrol
body: ### Bug Description Looks like the sync progress bar is based on completed folders: Of course, the Inbox is very large... and my organized folders were much smaller. So on first sync, the progress bar was artificially incorrect at 0% while the Inbox was syncing. It only started moving once the folders started to be processed. ### Steps to Reproduce Ok, well I wrote 'not applicable' here, but will write something. The bug reports is missing a typical question on 'expected behaviour' or 'what should be different', etc. Thinking that we should base the progress bar on total processed files or base it on processed file size of the emails. Not sure what options we have for tracking progress in terms of a) imap protocol or b) database information of already processed files, but thinking that just using Folders will result in a 'slow progress bar' even though massive amounts of files may be processed in a single folder, in this case, the Inbox. ### Application Version 2607.1 ### Installation Type Docker ### Management Tools Myself, prone to errors when editing files in VI. ### Host System Ubuntu ### Mail Provider Gmail ### Relevant Log Output ```shell N/A ```

title: [Bug]: Unable to see images
author: daxxio
body: ### Bug Description Hello, not sure if it's me who does something wrong. But I use your docker-compose without environment variables, except for the dbstrings, authentication & timezone. No images load at all. Is mailarchiver supposed to save images to the disk? I assumed it does, because the images exists inside of the html file. What could be wrong why my instance does not load any email's images. ### Steps to Reproduce 1) Setup like so: ``` services: mailarchive-app: image: s1t5/mailarchiver:latest restart: always environment: # Database Connection - ConnectionStrings__DefaultConnection=Host=postgres;Database=MailArchiver;Username=mailuser;Password=masterkey; # Authentication Settings - Authentication__Username=admin - Authentication__Password=secure123! # TimeZone Settings - TimeZone__DisplayTimeZoneId=Europe/Berlin ports: - "5000:5000" networks: - postgres volumes: - ./data-protection-keys:/app/DataProtection-Keys depends_on: postgres: condition: service_healthy postgres: image: postgres:17-alpine restart: always environment: POSTGRES_DB: MailArchiver POSTGRES_USER: mailuser POSTGRES_PASSWORD: masterkey volumes: - ./postgres-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data networks: - postgres healthcheck: test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U mailuser -d MailArchiver"] interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 start_period: 10s networks: postgres: ``` 2) Add a mail account and sync emails 3) Open up an email to see no images. ### Application Version 2607.1 ### Installation Type Docker ### Management Tools dockhand ### Host System Synology DS1821+ ### Mail Provider GMX.de ### Relevant Log Output ```shell no relevant logs... ```

title: [Bug]: Personal Outlook account showing as "not yet authorised" despite successful authorisation.
author: notmaroun
body: ### Bug Description Hello, when I go to edit the email account, it shows as Authorised - but when I check the account details: ...it shows as not yet authorised, and syncing doesn't work. I've tried to reauthorise twice - I'm unsure where to go from here. Both IMAP and POP are enabled on the email account. The email account itself is 294% full - so I'm trying to use this tool to archive the emails before I delete them off the server. I'm unsure if exceeding Outlook's email storage quota is an issue in this case. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Add a personal Outlook account and authorise it. 2. Check the status in the "edit" menu - it'll show as authorised. 3. Check the status in the "view" menu - it'll show as not yet authorised. 4. title: [Bug]:  IMAP sync fails with "unable to get certificate CRL" even though the server certificate is valid (free.fr)
author: ThomasMillochau
body:
### Bug Description

Syncing an IMAP account on imap.free.fr:993 fails with both SSL/TLS and STARTTLS, even though the server's certificate is valid and trusted (verified manually with openssl s_client, Verification: OK, Verify return code: 0 (ok)).
The actual rejection reason in the logs is:
The server's SSL certificate could not be validated for the following reasons:
- The server certificate has the following errors:
  • unable to get certificate CRL
  • unable to get certificate CRL
This points to a certificate revocation check failure (CRL/OCSP unreachable from inside the container), not an actual certificate trust problem. The .NET SslStream/X509Chain revocation check appears to be set to a mode that fails hard when the CRL distribution point can't be reached, rather than treating it as a soft failure (which is the more common/expected behavior, since CRL checking failures are common in containerized/restricted-network environments).


To Reproduce

Add an IMAP account using imap.free.fr, port 993, SSL/TLS.
Trigger a sync.
Sync fails with SslHandshakeException / AuthenticationException: The remote certificate was rejected by the provided RemoteCertificateValidationCallback.

Expected behavior

The connection should succeed, or at minimum, revocation-check failures (CRL/OCSP unreachable) should not cause a hard rejection of an otherwise valid certificate — similar to how MailSync__IgnoreSelfSignedCert handles self-signed certs, a separate option (or default behavior) to ignore revocation-check failures (X509RevocationMode.NoCheck or Online with soft-fail) would resolve this.
Environment

Mail-Archiver version: 2606.4
Deployment: Docker
IMAP provider: free.fr (imap.free.fr:993)

Logs
fail: MailArchiver.Services.MailSyncBackgroundService[0]
      Error syncing mail account .....@free.fr: Both SSL/TLS and STARTTLS connection attempts failed
      ...
      MailKit.Security.SslHandshakeException: An error occurred while attempting to establish an SSL or TLS connection.
      The server's SSL certificate could not be validated for the following reasons:
      • The server certificate has the following errors:
        • unable to get certificate CRL
        • unable to get certificate CRL
       ---> System.Security.Authentication.AuthenticationException: The remote certificate was rejected by the provided RemoteCertificateValidationCallback.

### Steps to Reproduce

Add an IMAP account using imap.free.fr, port 993, SSL/TLS.
Trigger a sync.
Sync fails with SslHandshakeException / AuthenticationException: The remote certificate was rejected by the provided RemoteCertificateValidationCallback.

### Application Version

2606.4

### Installation Type

Docker

### Management Tools

_No response_

### Host System

Debian 12

### Mail Provider

imap.free.fr

### Relevant Log Output

```shell
fail: MailArchiver.Services.MailSyncBackgroundService[0]
      Error syncing mail account ....@free.fr: Both SSL/TLS and STARTTLS connection attempts failed
      ...
      MailKit.Security.SslHandshakeException: An error occurred while attempting to establish an SSL or TLS connection.
      The server's SSL certificate could not be validated for the following reasons:
      • The server certificate has the following errors:
        • unable to get certificate CRL
        • unable to get certificate CRL
       ---> System.Security.Authentication.AuthenticationException: The remote certificate was rejected by the provided RemoteCertificateValidationCallback.
```

title: [Bug]: Search in to:/from: does not match domain part of email addresses
author: migro3466
body:
### Bug Description

Hi,

I think this might be related to #199, but the behavior seems different, so I’m opening a new issue.

### Problem

When searching within `to:` or `from:` fields, only the local part of an email address seems to be searchable.

### Observed behavior

* `to:alex` → ✅ works
* `to:alex@` → ✅ works
* `to:alex@e` → ❌ no results
* `to:example` → ❌ no results
* `to:example.com` → ❌ no results

Searching with the full email address still works correctly:

* `to:alex@example.com` → ✅ works

### Expected behavior

It would be expected that at least the domain part of the email address is searchable as well, e.g.:

* `to:example`
* `to:example.com`

### Question

Is this intended behavior (indexing only local-part), or is the domain not being indexed correctly?
If this is a regression, could it be related to the search index or require a rebuild after upgrading?

Thanks!


### Steps to Reproduce

see above

### Application Version

2606.4

### Installation Type

Docker

### Management Tools

_No response_

### Host System

_No response_

### Mail Provider

_No response_

### Relevant Log Output

```shell
n/a
```

title: [Bug]: Searching for "Umlauts" in HTML Mails does not seem to work
author: kladderadeng
body:
### Bug Description

Sorry for the 2nd post in one day. Just testing this Software, love it so far, but come across a few things I think I should mention.

I have HTML E-Mails imported that have Umlauts (like äöü) in them. I just found out, that I cannot search for words containing those Umlauts. After realizing that in the E-Mail Details page I can switch from HTML to Text, I think I know why, but do not know how to solve it.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Import HTML E-Mails containing Umlauts. It does not matter if you use the MBOX or EML format.
2. Try to Full-Text search for one of the words containing an Umlaut.
3. The search will yield no results.

I don't even know how to solve this. Probably on Import, if E-Mails containing HTML are not just added to the Search Index "as-is", but interpreted as HTML and the HTML output is put into the Search Index might solve this?

### Application Version

2606.3

### Installation Type

Docker

### Management Tools

No

### Host System

title: [Bug]: Timezone not honored for the Database Maintenance Task
author: kladderadeng
body: ### Bug Description I suppose it is a bug, so sorry in advance if it works as intended. For me, the time in the Container shows always as UTC when entering the Container. This also applies to the Database Maintenance Task, when activated via Docker Compose. I reside in UTC+0200 and set the Maintenance Task to run at 12:00 and in reality, it runs at 14:00. So 2 hours later. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Use Mail-Archiver 2606.3 with Docker Compose 2. Set 'DatabaseMaintenance__DailyExecutionTime=12:00' 3. Set TimeZone__DisplayTimeZoneId=Europe/Berlin (equals UTC+0200 at the moment) 4. Wait for the DatabaseMaintenance Job to run and check the Container Logs. It will run and state it ran at 12:00, but if you look in the GUI, it ran at 14:00. Since we set the Timezone to UTC+0200, it ran 2 hours too late. ### Application Version 2606.3 ### Installation Type Docker ### Management Tools No ### Host System Ubuntu 24.04 LTS Server ### Mail Provider None at the Moment ### Relevant Log Output ```shell ''' mailarchive-app-1 | info: MailArchiver.Services.AttachmentDeduplicationBackgroundService[0] mailarchive-app-1 | Attachment Deduplication migration already completed; nothing to do mailarchive-app-1 | info: MailArchiver.Services.DatabaseMaintenanceService[0] mailarchive-app-1 | Starting scheduled database maintenance at 06/27/2026 12:57:00 ''' ``` Let me add this Screenshot, so you can see both, the Docker Compose output and the Log within the Application: Now as writing this, the Timezone Parameter in Docker Compose reads as "Display Timezone", so maybe it works as intended... but this also means, when the Container runs in UTC, the Maintenance Job will run at different times, depending if it is Summer or Winter time.

title: Search ignores the full-text GIN index when sorting by date, so some searches take 20+ seconds
author: jwiegley
body:
I run mail-archiver against a Postgres database with about 250k archived emails, and lately searches were taking anywhere from a tenth of a second to 22 seconds, seemingly at random. I went digging, and it turns out the database isn't missing any index. There's a perfectly good full-text GIN index on the `to_tsvector(...)` expression, and `SearchEmailsOptimizedAsync` builds exactly the query to use it. The planner just doesn't use it.

The culprit is the `ORDER BY "SentDate" DESC LIMIT n` on the end. For any term Postgres estimates as common, it walks the `SentDate` index backward and checks the full-text match as a per-row filter, which means re-running `to_tsvector` over every detoasted body as it goes. When the matches happen to be recent, it finds the first page fast. When they're old or sparse, it walks most of the table.

Here's what `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` showed on my data:

- rare term, GIN index used: under 1 ms
- common term, recent matches, backward scan + filter: ~90 ms
- common-ish term, sparse or old matches, same plan: ~22 seconds

A few things make it worse:

- `ParseSearchTermForTsQuery` appends `:*` to every term. The prefix match inflates the planner's row estimate, which is exactly what pushes it toward the backward scan.
- The filter re-tokenizes the body on every row, and some of mine have >2047-char "words" (inline base64), so it's slow and noisy ("word is too long to be indexed").
- The GIN index isn't created by a migration, so unless you've added it by hand, search ends up scanning the whole table anyway.

I worked around it on my end with `ALTER ROLE ... SET enable_indexscan = off`, which forces the bitmap/GIN plan and keeps search under ~50 ms. But that's a blunt instrument, and it shouldn't be necessary.

A real fix could be any of these:

1. Fence the full-text match before the sort and limit: put the `WHERE ... @@ ...` in a subquery with `OFFSET 0`, then `ORDER BY ... LIMIT` the matched rows. That keeps the GIN index in play.
2. Add a `STORED` generated `tsvector` column with its own GIN index and search that. Then even a filter recheck reads a precomputed vector instead of re-tokenizing the body.
3. Don't append `:*` unconditionally. Only when prefix matching is actually wanted, or use `websearch_to_tsquery`.
4. Ship the GIN index as an EF migration so it's there by default.

Happy to test a patch against my archive if that helps.

Environment: s1t5/mailarchiver:latest (sha256:6843252b…), Postgres 16, ~250k emails.

title: [Bug]: Text below inline Image is cut off
author: rehajel
body: ### Bug Description Hi, when I'm viewing an Email that has an inline Image (sent via Apple Mail), the text below the image is cut off. The image itself is only shown as an attachment, but I'm fine with that. I suspect that this is not a general problem with all inline images but rather a problem with how apple mail handles this since other attachments and inline images from other mail software seem to work fine. I attached a screenshot from fastmail client and mail-archiver of the same mail to see the difference. Just as an aside: I also see this behavior in "bichon" - a rustmailer based email archiving software. "Openarchiver" seems to handle it as I would expect (showing text below the image). Thanks for providing this software and have a nice day :) -rehajel ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Send or receive an email with an attachment from an apple mail client (ios, macos, ipados...) 2. Archive mails for relevant mailbox 3. View sent/received email in mail-archiver. 4. Observe Cut Off text ### Application Version 2605.2 ### Installation Type Docker ### Management Tools Dockhand ### Host System Unraid 7.3.1 ### Mail Provider fastmail ### Relevant Log Output ```shell no relevant logs I think ```

title: [Bug]: Error reported on mailbox names starting with a dot / full stop / period
author: ADBeasley
body:
### Bug Description

Please note this may be a quirk with IMAP and older programs than I am currently using as this folder dates back to 2021 at least (possibly older).

I have had a bad habit of creating important mailbox names starting with a dot (full stop) as these are displayed first in the tree view of my mail program / web viewer on the ISP server.

The initial archive download and resync responds with an error - "EXAMINE mailbox does not exist".

Any folder underneath this 'dot' folder gets processed correctly.

Access to the 'dot' folder in my email client (Mac Mail) reports an error stating the folder name should not start with a dot.

Access to the 'dot' folder in the web client my ISP provides is fine!

There are mail messages in the 'dot' folder - these are archived and viewing them reports the folder name without the leading dot.

In my case I can rename this type of folder with no problem on-going


### Steps to Reproduce

1) Create a mailbox folder called Self
2) Create a mailbox folder within this called .ABCDEFG
3) Move a test email into this .ABCDEFG folder
4) Let archive task run / manually run full resync

### Application Version

2605.1

### Installation Type

Docker

### Management Tools

None

### Host System

Ubuntu 26.04 LTS

### Mail Provider

ISP mail - uses package called SmarterMail Enterprise Build 9518 (Jan 22, 2026)

### Relevant Log Output

```shell
Docker log line:
      Error syncing folder Self/.ABCDEFG: The IMAP server replied to the 'EXAMINE' command with a 'BAD' response: EXAMINE mailbox does not exist

(Note: ABCDEFG is anonymised - sample screen shots can be sent by email)
```
